title: Coronidium gnaphalioides
taxonomy: Coronidium gnaphalioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and is classified as a part of the genus Coronidium. The genus Coronidium is a group of small shrubs that are commonly known as "daisy bush." Coronidium gnaphalioides is often found in dry and sandy areas, where it grows as a low-lying shrub. It is classified within the subfamily Gnaphalieae, which is a group of daisy-like plants.

intro: Coronidium gnaphalioides, also known as Gnaphalium or Cudweed, is a small, annual plant native to Australia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Bioactive constituents of Coronidium gnaphalioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coronidium gnaphalioides is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to soothe and protect the skin.
